thread.title: Investors cover shorts ahead of Maharashtra election results, FIIs boost long positions; 24,000 key hurdle for Nifty
thread.title_full: Investors cover shorts ahead of Maharashtra election results, FIIs boost long positions; 24,000 key hurdle for Nifty

author: Shaleen Agrawal
title: Investors cover shorts ahead of Maharashtra election results, FIIs boost long positions; 24,000 key hurdle for Nifty
text: Investors rushed to cover short positions in NSE Nifty and Bank Nifty on November 22, ahead of the Maharashtra and Jharkhand election results, driving the highest unwinding of the November derivatives series. Nifty saw an unwinding of 12.23 lakh shares, while Bank Nifty recorded an unwinding of 1.35 lakh shares, with the rise in the indices confirming short covering. FIIs boosted net long positions in indices, with their long-short ratio jumping to 31 percent from 25 percent, the biggest single-day change in November. Key resistance for Nifty emerged at 24,000, with strong call writing at the strike, while significant put writing at 23,500 established it as a firm support. On November 22, Indian share market surged, fueled by oversold conditions and short-covering in a bearish phase. The rebound saw the benchmark equity indices ending up about 2.5 percent, with BSE Sensex jumping nearly 2,000 points and Nifty reclaiming 23,900. The unwinding in open positions and price gains across both indices signalled short covering, said Rajesh Palviya, Head - Technical and Derivatives, Axis Securities, adding that the FIIs’ positioning indicates “a cautiously optimistic sentiment in the market”. Nifty resistance has moved to 24,300-24,500, while a strong support zone has emerged at 23,500-23,300, he said. Also read | Bear market bounceback or robust rally? FII flows, global cues to decide future direction for Sensex, Nifty Further, Bank Nifty's pivotal level is at 51,000, with resistance continuing at 52,000, 51,500, and 52,500, said Palviya. On the downside, support has shifted to 51,000, 50,500, and 50,000. However, he cautioned that geopolitical developments or any unexpected election outcomes could influence sentiment. Bullish signals emerge in options market There was a shift seen towards bullish sentiment in the options market, said Dhupesh Dhameja, Derivatives Analyst at SAMCO Securities. The put-call ratio (PCR) rose to 1.15 from 0.85, hinting at growing bullish sentiment, he said. “Notable put option writing activity was recorded for the first time in two months, indicating a bullish stance among traders,” he added. Rising put option writing at strikes between 23,500 and 23,700 indicates strong buying interest at these levels, said Dhameja. The 24,000 strike saw the highest call option concentration, with 64.67 lakh contracts, showing significant resistance. “A decisive breakout above this level could trigger further short covering, driving the index toward 24,500, where the next hurdle lies,” Dhameja added. Also read | It’s a counter-rally, not a trend reversal: Rohit Srivastava The "max pain" level, which represents the point of maximum options expiry-related losses for traders, shifted to 23,700, suggesting limited downside in the short term. Dhameja recommended a "Buy on Dips" approach, provided the index holds above the key support zone of 23,500. Technical indicators align with short covering The technical outlook also supported the bullish momentum, with the Nifty regaining its 200-day simple moving average (DSMA). Osho Krishan, Senior Analyst - Technical and Derivative Research, Angel Broking, said that the 14-period RSI has moved out of oversold territory, aligning with historical support at the 200 DSMA. However, he cautioned against aggressive positioning. "The presence of lower highs remains a concern. Until we see a decisive and sustainable breakthrough above 24,000, it’s advisable to keep positions light," he said. Krishan identified key support for the Nifty at 23,600-23,500 and immediate resistance at 24,000-24,050. Market braces for state poll outcomes and expiry Looking ahead, analysts flagged the importance of the Maharashtra and Jharkhand election results over the weekend, along with the upcoming F&O monthly expiry. “On Monday, the market is most likely to react to the election outcome of Maharashtra and Jharkhand states, as a BJP win in both states may be a sentiment booster. However, the influence of the same on the market is likely to be very short-lived,” said Palviya. The real test for Nifty will be sustaining momentum above 24,000.
